name: pyfwup

metadata.name: pyfwup
metadata.summary: Python library for programming various USB bootloaders.

documentation:
# Python FirmWare UPloader

pyfwup (pronouned py-fwup; like the noise of packets flying over a wire) is a toolkit for interfacing with various microcontroller bootloaders. It is meant to allow firmware updates (and live programming) of various devices from your python tools.

It's also useful to provide a set of DFU-ish utilities that are platform-agnostic, that don't require a build environment, and that can be pulled down with simple 'pip' commands or as setup.py dependencies.

modules.name: fwup.errors
documentation: Generic error collection for pynucleus.
This file is part of pynucleus.

classes.name: fwup.errors.BoardNotFoundError
documentation: Class representing a condition where no board was found.

classes.name: fwup.errors.ProgrammingFailureError
documentation: Class representing a condition where programming failed.

modules.name: fwup.core
documentation: Core definitions for pyfwup.

classes.name: fwup.core.FwupTarget

methods.name: fwup.core.FwupTarget.__search_descendent_classes
documentation: Search all descendent classes for a class that meets the given condition.

methods.name: fwup.core.FwupTarget.from_utility_name
documentation: Returns the board type appropriate for the given utility name.

methods.name: fwup.core.FwupTarget.from_target_name
documentation: Returns the board type appropriate for the given utility name.

methods.name: fwup.core.FwupTarget._print_preconnect_info
documentation:
Prints any information that should be printed before a target object is connected.
Useful for cases where target waits for e.g. a button press.

This version should be overridden by the subclasses, where possible.
It's guaranteed to have a print_function that's callable.

methods.name: fwup.core.FwupTarget.print_preconnect_info
documentation:
Prints any information that should be printed before a target object is connected.
Useful for cases where target waits for e.g. a button press.

This version should be overridden by the subclasses, where possible.
It's guaranteed to have a print_function that's callable.

methods.name: fwup.core.FwupTarget._print_target_info
documentation:
Prints information about the relevant target, if possible.

This version should be overridden by the subclasses, where possible.
It's guaranteed to have a print_function that's callable.

methods.name: fwup.core.FwupTarget.print_target_info
documentation:
Prints information about the relevant target, if possible.

Base classes should prefer to override _print_board_info, as it will
always be passed a callable print_function.

methods.name: fwup.core.FwupTarget.size_to_program
documentation:
Returns the size to program for a given binary.
This function allows subclasses to override the size to be programmed, in case
it needs to e.g. always program full pages, or always program the whole flash.
Abstract base class for pyfwup supported boards. Allows use in fwup-util and derivatives.

modules.name: fwup.fx3
documentation: Programmer definition for loading images to the Cypress FX3 bootloader.
This file is part of pyfwup.

documentation: Creates a new connection to a given micronucleus bootloader.

methods.name: fwup.fx3.FX3Target._firmware_request
documentation: Transfers chunks of firmware to or from the device.

methods.name: fwup.fx3.FX3Target._read_firmware_chunk

methods.name: fwup.fx3.FX3Target._upload_firmware_chunk
documentation: Uploads a chunk of firmware to the device.

methods.name: fwup.fx3.FX3Target._upload_firmware
documentation: Uploads a chunk of firmware to the device.

methods.name: fwup.fx3.FX3Target._print_target_info
documentation: Prints information about the relevant board, if possible.

methods.name: fwup.fx3.FX3Target.erase
documentation: Erases the board's program flash.

methods.name: fwup.fx3.FX3Target.size_to_program
documentation: Computes the total size of the data to be programmed.

methods.name: fwup.fx3.FX3Target.program
documentation: Programs a given binary program to the microcontroller's flash memory.

methods.name: fwup.fx3.FX3Target.run_user_program

Class that allows one to program an FX3 bootloader target.

modules.name: fwup.dfu
documentation: DFU programming functionality.

classes.name: fwup.dfu.DFUError
documentation: Error representing a device-reported DFU error.

classes.name: fwup.dfu.DFUTarget

methods.name: fwup.dfu.DFUTarget.__find_dfu_interface_on_device
documentation:
Locates any DFU-compatible interfaces on any configuration the device has.

Returns a 2-tuple with <configuration value>, <interface number> if a DFU interface
exists on the device; or None, None if none exists.

methods.name: fwup.dfu.DFUTarget.__is_dfu_device
documentation: Returns true iff the given pyusb device is DFU-capable.

methods.name: fwup.dfu.DFUTarget.find_dfu_devices
documentation:
Returns a list of USB devices currently detected in DFU mode.
Accepts the same arguments as pyusb's usb.core.find(), allowing for specificity.

methods.name: fwup.dfu.DFUTarget.__init__
documentation:
Creates a new class representing a DFU target.

Accepts the same specifier arguments as pyusb's usb.core.find(); plus an index argument that gets
the Nth available device.

methods.name: fwup.dfu.DFUTarget.__read_device_info
documentation: Retrieve information from the DFU-capable device.

methods.name: fwup.dfu.DFUTarget.__parse_dfu_functional_descriptor
documentation: Parse device information from the DFU functional descriptor

methods.name: fwup.dfu.DFUTarget.__dfu_out_request
documentation: Convenience function that issues a DFU OUT control request to our device.

methods.name: fwup.dfu.DFUTarget.__dfu_in_request
documentation: Convenience function that issues a DFU IN control request, reading data from our device.

methods.name: fwup.dfu.DFUTarget.__get_status
documentation: Retrieve the device's current DFU status.

methods.name: fwup.dfu.DFUTarget.__complete_command
documentation: Blocks until the given command completes, checking status.

methods.name: fwup.dfu.DFUTarget.__raw_write_page
documentation:
Uploads a single page of data to the DFU target. On some targets, this requires that
the relevant memory be erased before applying.

methods.name: fwup.dfu.DFUTarget.__send_download_complete
documentation: Indicates that a DFU download is complete.

methods.name: fwup.dfu.DFUTarget.program
documentation: Uploads a given program to the target DFU device.

methods.name: fwup.dfu.DFUTarget.run_user_program
documentation: Runs the target user program.
Class that represents a target device in DFU mode.

modules.name: fwup.lpc43xx
documentation: LPC43xx programming functionality.

classes.name: fwup.lpc43xx.LPC43xxTarget

methods.name: fwup.lpc43xx.LPC43xxTarget.__init__
documentation: Finds DFU devices that specifically match NXP's LPC VID/PID.

methods.name: fwup.lpc43xx.LPC43xxTarget.program
documentation: Uploads a program to the LPC43xx's RAM.
Class representing an LPC43xx in DFU mode.

modules.name: fwup_utils.fwup_util
documentation: !/usr/bin/env python
Multi-firmware programming binary.

functions.name: fwup_utils.fwup_util.log_stderr
documentation: Helper to log to stderr.

functions.name: fwup_utils.fwup_util.log_null
documentation: Helper that discards input.

functions.name: fwup_utils.fwup_util.main
documentation: Simple programmer for pyfwup supported boards.

classes.name: fwup.micronucleus.MicronucleusBoard

methods.name: fwup.micronucleus.MicronucleusBoard.__init__
documentation: Creates a new connection to a given micronucleus bootloader.

methods.name: fwup.micronucleus.MicronucleusBoard._try_connect
documentation: Attempts to connect to a given micronucleus device. Useful for both connecting and reconnecting.

methods.name: fwup.micronucleus.MicronucleusBoard.reconnect
documentation: Attempts to reconnect to the given board.

methods.name: fwup.micronucleus.MicronucleusBoard._validate_device_compatibility
documentation: Returns true iff the given pyUSB device is a a micronucleus board we can program.

methods.name: fwup.micronucleus.MicronucleusBoard._request_in
documentation: Requests data from the Micronucleus device.

methods.name: fwup.micronucleus.MicronucleusBoard._request_out
documentation: Issues data to the Micronucleus device, or issues a command without data.

methods.name: fwup.micronucleus.MicronucleusBoard._populate_info_from_device
documentation: Populates this object with information about the target device, and its bootloader.

methods.name: fwup.micronucleus.MicronucleusBoard._print_target_info
documentation: Prints information about the relevant board, if possible.

methods.name: fwup.micronucleus.MicronucleusBoard.erase
documentation: Erases the board's program flash.

methods.name: fwup.micronucleus.MicronucleusBoard.program
documentation: Programs a given binary program to the microcontroller's flash memory.

methods.name: fwup.micronucleus.MicronucleusBoard._extract_reset_address
documentation: Attempt to extract the reset address of the relevant program from the program data's first page.

methods.name: fwup.micronucleus.MicronucleusBoard._patch_first_page
documentation: Patches a jump-to-bootloader into the first page of the user program.

methods.name: fwup.micronucleus.MicronucleusBoard._patch_last_page
documentation:
Patches the user reset address into the last double-word of the user program.
Used by the bootloader to identify the location of the relevant target.

methods.name: fwup.micronucleus.MicronucleusBoard._patch_in_jump
documentation:
Patches a given data buffer to contain a jump instruction.

Params:
    data_buffer -- The bytearray buffer to be modified.
    offset      -- The current offset in the bytearray buffer.
    jump_target -- The target of the relevant jump

methods.name: fwup.micronucleus.MicronucleusBoard.__raw_write_page
documentation:
Writes a page to the microcontroller's firmware. This should never be used directly!
Instead, call program(), which handles reset vector redirection to ensure the bootloader
runs correctly each time.

methods.name: fwup.micronucleus.MicronucleusBoard.__raw_write_page_v2
documentation: Variant of raw_write_page for the micronucleus v2 protocol.

methods.name: fwup.micronucleus.MicronucleusBoard.__raw_write_page_v1
documentation:
Writes a page to the microcontroller's firmware. This should never be used directly!
Instead, call program(), which handles reset vector redirection to ensure the bootloader
runs correctly each time.

methods.name: fwup.micronucleus.MicronucleusBoard.get_cpu_name
documentation: Returns a short description of the relevant board's main processor.

methods.name: fwup.micronucleus.MicronucleusBoard.run_user_program
documentation: Requests that the currently-programmed user application be executed.
Class that allows one to program a Micronucleus bootloader target.
